target.title: fix(tui): surface visible message-tool replies and restore backgrounded runs on session switch-back
target.body:
## Overview

Two related terminal-TUI / WebChat message-delivery **display** fixes (this branch's two
commits). Both are source-level, client-side, and additive — no gateway/protocol/persistence or
dependency-surface changes. (This is the source-level redo of the dist-bundle patch script that
was closed in #87787 with the guidance to fix the TypeScript source instead.)

### Review feedback addressed
- **Rebased** onto current `origin/main` (was diverged; now 0 behind / 2 ahead).
- **Global-agent scoping fixed (was the [P1] finding):** the `global` session key is shared
  across agents, and rendering/history already scope it by `agentId` (`isMatchingGlobalAgentEvent`).
  The live-run store now keys global runs by `global:<agentId>` so a backgrounded run from one
  agent can no longer be restored into another agent's TUI on switch-back. Added regression tests
  on both the retention and restore sides (each proven to fail without the scoping).

---

## Issue 1 — visible message-tool replies hidden behind control acknowledgements

### Root cause (verifiable in source)
When the agent replies via a routeless `message(action="send")`, the reply is delivered to the
source conversation, but the visible chat (history projection + live TUI) rendered the model's
internal control acknowledgement (`"Visible reply sent."` / `NO_REPLY`) or a trailing
final-summary turn instead of the delivered reply. Successful message-tool sends were not
consistently mirrored into the projected history, and streamed/final assistant text rendered
before the delivered reply surfaced.

### Fix
- `src/gateway/control-reply-text.ts`: add `SUPPRESSED_CONTROL_REPLY_PHRASES` (`Visible reply
  sent.`) so the phrase + lead fragments are treated as suppressed control text.
- `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts`: after a delivered message-tool reply, surface the
  real reply and drop the summary turn (`hasSucceededPendingMirror`); add shared
  `readVisibleMessageToolReplyFromArgs`.
- `src/tui/tui-formatters.ts`: `isControlAcknowledgementMessage` drops control-only assistant
  finals from TUI display.
- `src/tui/tui-event-handlers.ts`: track per-run message-tool visible replies, render the
  delivered reply on final, and project/suppress live delta text.

---

## Issue 2 — backgrounded in-flight run invisible on session switch-back

### Root cause (verifiable in source)
The TUI is a single gateway client that receives events for **all** of its sessions but renders
only the active one. Switching agents/sessions is purely client-side:
- `setSession` (`src/tui/tui-session-actions.ts`) sets `state.activeChatRunId = null` and reloads
  history; it does **not** abort the gateway run, so the run keeps executing in the background.
- `handleChatEvent`/`handleAgentEvent` early-returned for any event whose `sessionKey` is not the
  active session (`src/tui/tui-event-handlers.ts`, the `if (!isSameSessionKey(...)) return` guard),
  and `syncSessionKey` resets the per-run `TuiStreamAssembler` on switch.
- `loadHistory` rebuilds the chat log only from **persisted** history; an in-flight run's streamed
  text is not yet persisted.

→ On return, the backgrounded run's accumulated partial and its activity status are gone, and the
history reload cannot show them. (Reviewer can verify each step against the cited functions.)

### Fix (client-only)
A per-session live-run store retains in-flight runs from the events the client already receives,
and switch-back restores them:
- new `src/tui/tui-live-run-store.ts`: `Map<storeKey, TuiStreamAssembler>`, self-pruning when a
  session's last run terminates (the assembler's run ids ARE the in-flight set; no size cap).
  `liveStoreSessionKey()` scopes the shared `global` session by agent (`global:<agentId>`);
  non-global keys already encode their agent and pass through unchanged.
- `src/tui/tui-stream-assembler.ts`: `runIds()` + `getDisplayText(runId, showThinking)`.
- `src/tui/tui-event-handlers.ts`: `routeChatEventToLiveStore` updates the store for every chat
  event; backgrounded sessions update the store then return instead of being dropped; terminal
  events drop the run.
- `src/tui/tui-session-actions.ts`: after the history replay, `restoreLiveSession` re-renders the
  in-flight run (partial + `streaming`) and re-adopts `activeChatRunId`.
- `src/tui/tui.ts`: one shared store wired into both factories.

Alternatives considered and rejected: a gateway `chat.history` active-run snapshot and
server-side in-flight persistence — both add protocol/persistence surface for a client display
concern, so the client-only retention was chosen.

---

## Verification (exact commands run; nothing breaks)

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/tui/
  -> Test Files 23 passed (23) / Tests 443 passed (443)
     (adds global-session scoping coverage: runs are retained/restored per agent, no cross-agent leak)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts
  -> Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 14 passed (14)
     (adds e2e "restores a backgrounded in-flight run when switching back to its session")

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts \
  src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts src/tui/tui-formatters.test.ts
  -> Test Files 3 passed (3) / Tests 200 passed (200)   (Issue 1 coverage on current main)

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json
  -> no errors in touched files

oxfmt --check  and  oxlint   -> clean on touched files
```

## Real behavior proof (Issue 2)

Behavior addressed: a backgrounded in-flight run was invisible on switch-back in the terminal TUI; on return the session showed only persisted history, not the still-running run's partial or its activity status.

Real environment tested: the fake-backend PTY e2e drives the real `runTui()` terminal loop in an isolated TUI process over a PTY (real terminal rendering; the model endpoint is faked via `TuiBackend`). It parks on session `alpha` with a run that streams a partial and never finalizes, switches to session `beta`, then back to `alpha`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
OPENCLAW_TUI_PTY_MIRROR_PATH=/tmp/tui-switchback-mirror.txt \
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts \
  -t "restores a backgrounded in-flight run"
```

Evidence after fix: de-ANSI excerpt of the captured real `runTui()` terminal mirror (console output from the PTY), showing the in-flight partial and `streaming` status reappear on switch-back:
```
session agent:main:alpha
  PTY_HANG_PARTIAL: hang prompt          <- in-flight partial rendered on alpha
  streaming  0s | local ready            <- activity status = streaming
session agent:main:beta                  <- switched away; alpha's partial cleared
session agent:main:alpha                 <- switched back
  PTY_HANG_PARTIAL: hang prompt          <- partial RESTORED after switch-back
  streaming  0s | local ready            <- streaming status RESTORED

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 13 skipped (14)
```

Observed result after fix: on switch-back to session `alpha`, the backgrounded run's partial (`PTY_HANG_PARTIAL: hang prompt`) and its `streaming` activity status reappear in the live TUI. Before the fix the returned session rendered neither, because `loadHistory` rebuilds only from persisted history and the in-flight streamed text is not yet persisted.

What was not tested: a live gateway-backed (real transport) switch-away/return capture is not included — per `src/tui/CLAUDE.md` the fake-backend PTY harness does not prove Gateway transport, embedded backend runtime, providers, session persistence, or live streaming. For Issue 1, a fresh live WebChat/Telegram delivery capture is not included.
target.author: williamliu-ai
